Open Badge Factory as an alternative to Credly. 

Open Badge Factory is a digital badge maker that also deserves your attention. This alternative to Credly can be used by small companies, which is a big plus, as with Certifier. In addition, you can work on a variety of badges alone or with a team, as this tool provides you with unique collaborative features.

### Best features of this alternative to Credly

OPF, as Credly alternative, has many necessary functions for managing electronic badges so that you can use it successfully to validate competencies and achievements, motivate employees, volunteers, and students, set up learning pathways, monitor individual or company development goals. Moreover, the application is easy to use and intuitive. [...] Our last but not least suggestion is Canvas Credentials from Instructure. Canvas Credentials is an inclusive digital credentialing network. It allows you to issue badges to everyone, not just your employees or students, for their learning outcomes.

### Best features of this alternative to Credly

Canvas Credentials provides customization, documentation, training, analytics, data security, badge management, and much more. It's not just a seamless way for skills and achievement validation.

What's more, Instructure has more learning products in its portfolio that you can take advantage of. [...] Open Badges provides a versatile, secure, and easy-to-use way of quickly implementing your badges and credentials. If you have no experience with creating such online badges, you don't need to worry, as it will explain in simple steps how to take the first steps.

### Open Badge’s pricing – cost-effective Credly alternative

The platform provides five plans. Starter, Small, Medium, Large and Enterprise. The first plan, Starter, is free (with considerable limitations), and you can create certifications for up to 10 badge recipients and unlimited badges PA with this plan. The paid plans start at $55 per month. The Enterprise plan comes with custom billing.

A free plan is available for this Credly alternative.

results.title: Ultimate Guide to Badge Entry Systems for Employee Access
results.content:
## A better alternative to badge entry systems

It’s true that key card security systems for businesses are more convenient and cost-effective than hiring security guards to check every tenant’s ID card. However, relying on an ID badge entry system isn’t the best solution for property access. Instead, consider a mobile access control system that allows tenants to use their smartphones as credentials. [...] Smartphone-based entry systems maintain the same security as ID badge entry systems but present a more convenient entry method for tenants. Tenants can even grant visitors access remotely from a mobile app. Furthermore, building staff won’t waste time or money replacing or reprogramming ID badges. And tenants will be relieved that they don’t have to keep track of a small employee ID card for property access.

Invest in a mobile access control system consisting of:

A video intercom is a more robust alternative to badge entry systems. 

A video intercom is a more robust alternative to badge entry systems. 

### Video Intercom for your property entrance [...] As a modern key card and key fob system, badge systems are an easy replacement for traditional locks and keys. In this guide, we review what badge door entry systems are and how they work. Then, we cover the pros and cons of badge entry systems. Finally, we offer a simpler, more reliable alternative to badge entry systems.

In this guide, we cover:

ButterflyMX CTA

ButterflyMX CTA

## What is a badge entry system?

A badge entry system is an electronic building access control solution that authorizes access in and out of your building using a person’s ID badge as the credential. So, the badge is used for both visual identification and access authorization. As such, this type of system is popular with commercial and industrial properties.

results.title: Badge access control systems: A comprehensive guide | Envoy
results.content:
When a proximity card is brought near a reader, it transmits its unique identification number via radio waves, which the reader then processes to grant or deny access. While widely used, these cards can be vulnerable to cloning and relay attacks. As security needs grow, many organizations are exploring more secure alternatives.

### Magnetic stripe cards

Magnetic stripe cards are an older piece of technology that allows individuals to swipe their cards through a reader to gain access. Over time, these cards can wear out, making them less reliable. While still in use in some places, they offer less security compared to modern alternatives.

### Smart cards [...] 1. Mobile credentialing. With mobile access, employees can enter secure areas using their smartphones, eliminating the need for physical badges or keycards. This enhances convenience and boosts security by leveraging the biometric features already built into modern smartphones, such as fingerprint or facial recognition. As businesses become more mobile-centric, this technology ensures that lost or forgotten badges won’t disrupt operations or compromise security. [...] —

Want to learn how to improve workplace security? Check out our Workplace Security Kit. It's packed with resources to help you develop, implement, and manage your security policies from start to finish.

How this works with Envoy

Instead of relying on physical badges, Envoy lets you manage access digitally. Employees and visitors can sign in using their smartphones, making access faster and more secure. Envoy also integrates with other security tools, like video surveillance systems and identity screening software, for more complete control of who’s in your workplace.
